explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while fats are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 48 from a base nutrition score of 48. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: easy protein delivery for normal diets
topPros.explanation: Dairy gets real credit for convenience.

topPros.title: clear calcium identity
topPros.explanation: That is a core category strength.

topPros.title: slots into meals very easily
topPros.explanation: Practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

topCons.title: very dense and easy to overshoot
topCons.explanation: The nutrient density is real, but so is the concentration.

topCons.title: fat quality varies wildly across dairy
topCons.explanation: Some dairy is clean, some really is not.

topCons.title: processing changes the category a lot
topCons.explanation: The label alone does not tell the whole story.

closing.summary: Parmesan does have some solid upside, especially easy protein delivery for normal diets and clear calcium identity. but very dense and easy to overshoot and fat quality varies wildly across dairy keep it from climbing higher. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
